findings.note: handle_initialize returns protocolVersion=1 (integer), agentInfo.name='recursive', agentCapabilities with 8 keys. Unit test initialize_has_protocol_version_and_agent_info and agent_capabilities_has_flat_keys confirm shape. agentCapabilities keys ≥5 (8 actual).

All 8 forward-capability keys declared: promptCapabilities, toolCallNotifications, loadSession, resume, fs.readTextFile, fs.writeTextFile, mcpCapabilities (all truthy objects {}), terminalCapabilities (explicitly false). Unit test agent_capabilities_has_flat_keys asserts all 8 with correct truthy/falsy values.

run_stdio loop processes lines sequentially via BufReader::new(reader).lines(). Unit test three_initialize_requests_fifo sends 3 sequential initialize requests (ids 1,2,3), verifies 4 lines: success(id:1) + initialized notification + error(id:2) + error(id:3). All have .jsonrpc=='2.0', matching ids, no extra lines.

Module doc (lines 14-17) explicitly declares stdout/stderr contract. write_line writes JSON-RPC only to writer (stdout). eprintln! used for ServerShutdown. Unit test every_stdout_line_is_valid_jsonrpc verifies all stdout lines parse as valid JSON with .jsonrpc=='2.0'. E2E test 01-acp-initialize.yaml separately asserts stderr is non-empty.

Malformed JSON triggers serde_json::from_str error → build_error(Value::Null, PARSE_ERROR=-32700, 'Parse error: invalid JSON'). Unit test unclosed_brace_returns_parse_error sends '{bad json', verifies id=null, error.code=-32700, non-empty message. parse_error_then_valid_request_process_survives verifies server stays alive and accepts next valid initialize.

Unknown method: sync dispatch HashMap misses → falls to dispatch_async → '_' arm returns build_error(id, METHOD_NOT_FOUND=-32601). Unit tests before_init_unknown_method_returns_method_not_found and unknown_method_then_initialize_still_works confirm: (1) unknown method before init returns -32601, not -32002; (2) process survives and accepts valid initialize after.

dispatch_one validates jsonrpc field first (line 856: None → INVALID_REQUEST=-32600), then method field (line 874: None → INVALID_REQUEST). Unit tests json_without_jsonrpc_returns_invalid_request and missing_method_returns_invalid_request confirm both cases produce error.code=-32600. Process stays alive by design (loop continues reading lines after error).

dispatch_one line 900: env.id is None → returns (None, vec![]). process_line line 819: if response is None, write_line is skipped. Unit test notification_produces_no_response: send notification + valid request, verify only 2 lines (init response + initialized), no spurious id=null response. notification_alone_produces_nothing confirms zero output for solo notification.

run_stdio wraps lines.next_line() in tokio::time::timeout(60s). On elapsed: eprintln!('ServerShutdown') → break. Unit test idle_timeout_shuts_down_server_after_60s uses tokio::time::pause/advance(61s) and verifies function returns cleanly. idle_timeout_does_not_fire_before_60s confirms timeout does not fire at 30s. Test output shows 'ServerShutdown' on stderr.

No explicit SIGTERM handler in AcpServer. On SIGTERM, the OS default signal handler terminates the process with exit code 143 (128+15). Since no cleanup writes to stdout, no stale/truncated JSON is emitted. Contract accepts exit code 143. Gap: no unit or E2E test explicitly verifies SIGTERM behavior; relies on OS default semantics. Recommend adding an integration test that sends SIGTERM and verifies exit code ∈ {0,143}.

Code review: BufReader::new(reader).lines() at line 725; HashMap dispatch table at lines 169-172 (build_sync_dispatch_table, only 'initialize' mapped); loop body in run_stdio (lines 728-761, ~33 lines) delegates to process_line → dispatch_one. Zero references to AgentRuntime/run_inner/Provider/Session inside run_stdio loop body — those appear only in handle_session_new (line 395) and dispatch_async (line 180 comment), outside the loop. Sync dispatch table is a clean HashMap<&str, fn>. No explicit .lock() on stdout (tokio::io::stdout() is internally synchronized; equivalent async pattern).

E2E test exists at e2e/tests/01-acp-initialize.yaml, listed in e2e/e2e.yaml line 58 as 'ACP Initialize Handshake (Sprint 1)'. Test: (1) starts recursive acp via timeout wrapper, (2) pipes initialize JSON, (3) captures stdout lines, (4) runs jq assertions on protocolVersion, agentInfo.name, all 8 agentCapabilities keys, (5) verifies initialized notification, (6) verifies all stdout lines are valid JSON-RPC, (7) verifies stderr non-empty. Run via: argusai -c e2e.yaml -s acp-init.
